summary: cursed-modules ships install-time C2 backdoor and credential exfiltration
details: cursed-modules@999.0.7 is a malicious npm package that executes attacker-controlled code at install and require time. package.json registers install.js as preinstall, install, and postinstall, so `npm install` opens a polling C2 channel to 154.57.164.70:32447, beacons host metadata, then loops GETting commands from /api/modules/ECT-654321 and executing them via child_process.execSync, PUTting results back to /api/modules/ECT-987654 — an interactive remote shell on the installer. The same install.js collects /root/.ssh/id_rsa, /etc/hosts, the full process.env, the output of `id`, and a recursive filesystem walk, then PUTs them base64-encoded to the same host. index.js (the package main, executed on `require()`) additionally runs `aws sts get-caller-identity`, queries the EC2 instance metadata service at 169.254.169.254 for IAM credentials, runs `aws secretsmanager list-secrets`, and exfiltrates the results to 154.57.164.70:32447. recon.js targets npm supply-chain pivots specifically, reading /root/.npmrc, project-local.npmrc files, and verdaccio config.yaml + htpasswd, and PUTting them to http://154.57.164.76:30728/api/modules/ECT-654321. Both install.js and index.js gate execution on `os.hostname()` matching the 12-hex-character Docker container ID pattern and exit if scanner-sandbox markers are present, deliberately evading analysis environments. The 999.0.7 version, the bundled publish-and-arm.sh / cleanup-npm.sh scripts, and the embedded manifest strings (`ship_deck: dependency-confusion`, `cargo_hold: verdaccio-proxy`) confirm the package is part of a deliberate dependency-confusion campaign against internal verdaccio registries.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
